uh... very very recently uh... friend of mine called me up and told me a story it was a true story it was about uh... two business owners two entrepreneurs that they had their business in a small city and actually their businesses were right next to each other in the downtown area of the city and one of them was a dentist and the other one sold cell phones and uh... and cell services and their their businesses were right next to each other and in front of their business there was a bench and every day around twelve thirty to one o'clock they would get together and they would have lunch together sort of uh... they developed a relationship a camaraderie and they've they talked about life and business and things in general and recently and it's a true story recently the cell phone operator said to the dentist says doc how's your practice doing today and the doctor said i'm not doing so well i haven't been doing well as normal because i've been affected by the economy just as your cell phone store has and uh... the cell phone operator said to the to the dentist he says well what are you doing to change that and the dentist says i'm doing nothing and the cell phone guy looked at him and says you know it's interesting doc yesterday i asked you that question and uh... you told me the same answer you said i'm i'm doing nothing and the doctor looked at him and said i'm not finished yet and uh... and that's what a lot of entrepreneurs are actually doing right now they're not finished yet doing nothing and what we're doing here in these next four days is we're going basis so he missed those this morning you might wanna we're supposed to talk about the recession worrying about that at the moment seems sort of anti-climactic now that we have you know deadly pig flu and i said when we put the list up there of all the people from all those countries who've joined us i said to lead just imagine the diversity of viruses and diseases that they've brought into the room with them from from from all of those places that the rest of us are immune systems are completely uh... ill-equipped you know to uh... to uh... deal with so uh... one to make all you feel welcome and uh... uh... you know we got the poison food and we got the i'm sure you saw the news now that the drinking water is all full of drugs and narcotics and disposed of prozac and you know all of that which if you've looked at the water out of the tap here if you haven't pour yourself a glass of water to sit for a couple minutes and uh... uh... you'll notice uh... we have you know i ran in north korea and we even have pirates so it's a pirates uh... uh... it seems like uh... it seems like worrying about the recession is sort of you know almost pass a uh... but uh... but it was it was the topic assigned so it's the topic will do uh... and the first thing i would say to you about it is is that uh... i'm going to give you the three ways to get out of it the three things to do the the three marketing things to do to basically make a recession irrelevant to you uh... but but sort of has preface what i would say is that the uh... the the death of consumer spending uh... is greatly exaggerated uh... if you if you pay attention that news all the time you miss most of the other news uh... you would you can still go i'm sure many of you've had the experience uh... you can still go to a best buy parking lot and there's cars there uh... this by the way is not what was going on during the great depression everything i've read indicates nobody was over at best by waiting in line to buy an iphone uh... so similarities between where we are now and where we were then seemed to be greatly exaggerated in most business categories consumer spending is often single digit percentages there are exceptions there are geographic pockets there are certain product that service categories that are harder hit than others but for the most part we're talking about single digit percentages of drops uh... the interestingly the middle of the market uh... in most markets is hit hardest right so if you think restaurants the middle chains the apple chilies olive fridays those guys uh... well i say it that way because it would be see their problem with the recession is exposed really in many businesses are fundamental weaknesses that were there anyway and uh... and we needed this sort of animal to to flush some of it out of our system we really don't need eight restaurants diagonally crossing each other that are identical except for the name on the door and so this sort of saying this is caught up with people uh... but for the most part there's still a lot of money moving there's still a lot of money being spent and it's there for you to get if you wish to get it uh... so you ought not really be focusing on the percentage that spending is down in a category you ought to be focusing on the spending that is still of course uh... continuing and therefore you to get there's two things to think about there's the ability to buy and there's the willingness to buy so ability to buy compromised certain people certain segments of the population absolutely required uh... to restrain their spending in ways that they were not before that exists uh... it's very real uh... for both the fact that they may have less money or they may have less credit and if they've been doing all their spending with credit they are affected so willingness to buy is legitimately affected or ability far more affected is their willingness to buy their mental attitude about spending their emotional attitude about spending even whether or not they wish to be seen spending their ability to buy you can't do much about so spending a whole lot of time monitoring those statistics and listening to that news and paying attention to compromise the ability to buy is not very productive because there's not much you can do about it other than find ways not to be dealing with those people whose ability to buy is significantly compromised uh... but willingness to buy that we can do a lot about because that's psychological it's not practical or monetary it has to do with how the consumer is thinking and feeling about themselves about the economy about the products and services they buy in the vendors that they buy them from so we should put all of our focus or nearly all of our focus on this issue of will they buy not really can they buy or can't they buy and their willingness to buy has been compromised in many different ways which we'll talk about as we go along this this morning some are being more thoughtful some feel they should appear to be more responsible some feel they should appear not to be irresponsible uh... this is a window sign from a very successful high-end fashion store in new york i like it a lot uh... cashmere sweater twenty five hundred bucks the recession price twenty five hundred bucks uh... lambs fleece jacket eleven thousand dollars uh... recession price eleven thousand dollars uh... it was it was the merchant making a statement about everybody sort of stampede to discounting has the first and foremost reaction to any piece of bad economic news interestingly they have had a small number of people pretty much every day come in express their disapproval of the sign uh... and how insensitive inappropriate it is uh... they have had a larger number of people coming in to praise the sign and buy things uh... at full price and so even though generally speaking luxury goods retailers particularly in manhattan uh... are suffering some diminishment of sales right now this particular store sales are up uh... proving amongst other things my axiom that if you don't offend somebody by noon every day you're not really doing much at all uh... and so offending people uh... is profitable for him so let's talk about why people still by during the recession why don't they just stop all together it's a little clammy up here uh... why don't they stop some people continue to buy because they have to right because of necessity or perceived necessity uh... and so kids are going to school kids have to have shoes we're buying shoes recession no recession we may not by shoes for ourselves but we're going to buy shoes for the kids so one is necessity that again is not all that useful to you uh... because of course necessities tend to be commoditized uh... the other reason people keep buying during a recession is that their personal interests and passions always take precedence over pocketbook as a governing device everybody continues to spend on something the percentage of poor people who smoke is five times higher than the percentage of affluent people who smoke i suppose from the standpoint of the costs of long-term lifetime health care in america that's good news for those of us paying the bills but that's neither in or there so five times more poor people smoke what's the pack of cigarettes cost now well how much six bucks i got six bucks over here she knows anybody is anybody got a higher number eight k eight bucks over there see you should be buying your smokes where she's getting her sick as make a big difference over a year's time right so eight bucks six bucks a pack i have this sort of schizophrenic life half of my life is like with you guys and with business people half of my life is at the racetrack with track people and and most of them don't make much money most of them by uh... by most people standards are poor a huge number of them smoke none of them are quitting surely the first thing you would do in a recession if you are cash tight if you really don't have money is you'd stop smoking why don't they well because to them to that person that smoking is his so everybody has at least one thing on which they for which they will not stop spending no matter what now ideally you don't want to be selling to people whose thing is an eight dollar thing or an eight hundred dollar thing or an eight thousand dollar thing but the point is everybody here has something i mean god forbid if things got really really really really really bad and you were burning your furniture in the fireplace in the winter for heat uh... there's something you'd still be spending money on that was not a necessity but it was your thing i saw a guy three nights ago trucks parked the track got one tire blown out it's been sitting there for a while with one tire blown out and i kind of kidded him about how long it had been sitting there he said yes at the biggest inconvenience he didn't say inconvenience but he referred to pain in an anatomical part but he uh... but but he said that he said the biggest inconvenience is now that springs here two or three times a week i gotta get somebody to drive me to the golf course to play golf i know it's early but now think about this there's a truck over here which is his primary means of transportation which needs a tire how much is a tire i don't happen to know how much is a tire fifty bucks give or take cheap basic tire can you still buy when i had all bad cars you could buy used ones can you still buy recap tires so you could so he could buy a recap tire right around the golf of the municipal courses twenty three times a week he's getting somebody to give him a lift to the golf course one week he could have a tire but which is he going to give up a week of golf or his transportation it tells you a lot and he is not an isolated case by any stretch of the imagination the third reason people keep buying during the recession is really the one we can do the most about they keep buying because somebody actually makes an attempt at selling them something now this is a this is becoming a revolutionary concept because pretty much everybody's in surrender mode they like aren't trying to sell because they think nobody's buying so what happens in every recession it starts with big dumb companies and then it filters down they cut back on their advertising marketing and sales budgets and efforts in order to save money on the premise that nobody's buying i just saw yesterday anheuser-borscht notified NBC that they'll be spending only half as much as they have spent before on advertising during the olympics for beer official beer of the olympians uh... the uh... not one either one of two things is true either that advertising is stupid and wasteful to start with or the people running anheuser-borscht are imbeciles you take your pack actually you could put them together if you like but if the advertising is not stupid and wasteful on a regular basis then cutting it in half because there's a receipt that's not the thing to cut you want to get out there and spend more to sell more beer and spend more than your competitors because maybe you can get somebody to switch from the beer to your beer now well the other guys not spend as much but everybody starts to cut back on selling so i say you can't fix stupid so linkin linkin uh... linkin benefit life is a company uh... which which we bought long-term care insurance from and so every so often they send you a statement mine's prepaid i don't pay payments is prepaid so every so often you get a statement just kind of letting you know they're still around telling you you're paid up which i already knew but i think quarterly they send this statement which is basically says we're still here which i didn't used to wonder about but now you know you do so it's it's kind of nice getting it in the mail and says we're still here and that's good it gets course it's hopefully going to be a while before i need it so it's nice to know they're still there what they used to put in with the statement was stuffed to sell other stuff they're called statement stuffers to do cross-selling and so there used to be like a picture with the statement for some last two statements guess what no pitch so some being counter is sat in a room and decided since nobody will buy anything now anyway let's save the cost statement stuffers we just and very grudgingly because you couldn't have tried to undo this sale with any more vigor than these guys did but we just had the whiz bang turn your closet into your show place guys come out and do the closets in one of our homes so they were there in a plain white van with no signage parked twice in a neighborhood where houses are in a circle now seemingly you want like a big sign don't you? my guess is new van extra 200 bucks 300 bucks for the sign no attempt to upsell anything you're only doing one closet probably a second closet in the house somewhere odds are probably a room maybe building shelves something no attempt whatsoever no referral request no gee let's invite not that this would have worked with me but it will work with some people you know no let's let's invite your neighbors over for the break the bottle of champagne over the middle shelf you know disembarkation beginning of the new closet's life celebration party here's the pre-done invitations and the nothing no attempt to sell nothing we have a local restaurant pretty popular restaurant doing very very well it's independent run by a very nice couple they just closed for two weeks in order to go to Italy on vacation didn't seem real bright to me cause they got a bunch of customers that they took about two years ago when they opened from a restaurant that is down the street that's been there since the beginning of time and now being closed for two weeks my guess is a bunch of people go back to the other one and they give them another shot so but as the owner said there's nobody we would dare leave in charge for two weeks he pointed out to me that when I am there and he's gone for fifteen minutes it's a disaster so imagine what would happen in two weeks so I understand his managerial pain but no promotion prior to no promotion setting up the return no selling whatsoever so one of the reasons that spending is down is cause selling is down that's why a lot of spending is down is because selling is down nobody's really trying based upon the premise that nobody's buying so they put themselves on their own little catch twenty two and they are convinced they can't get out one of the reasons people do buy during the recession is somebody takes a whack selling them something let's talk about the three ways to get out if you are affected we'll go through them one by one one is to sell to someone different the second one is to sell differently and the third is to sell something different those are really your three choices when faced with an economic downturn so selling to somebody different there are really two types of customers who are pretty eager to buy right now almost every customer will buy given the right circumstances but there's two kinds of customers that are particularly cool to concentrate on what are what i would call opportunists their value buyers and so they've been contemplating doing something putting in a closet buying an RV whatever getting hair transplants whatever they've been contemplating and now they see this as an opportunity it could be pointed out to them this is an opportunity they can buy something they thought they couldn't afford because now it's at a better price than it was before so they're value buyers and you can communicate to them with a value message then there are my personal favorite group is what i would call the recession resistors the people who are motivated by that sign in the retail window the people who are sort of defiant about the whole thing and i'll be damned if i'll give up my fine wine or my dinner out or my whatever um... and they literally will buy to validate their belief that this is temporary and that this only affects the weak and the dumb it doesn't affect me and so they will buy because of that sort of emotional validation now sometimes you have to make it sort of possible for them uh... i'm told that the stores on rodeo drive in beveley hills uh... right now uh... huge upswing in the number of people who wish what they buy wish to have what they buy delivered to their homes uh... why the increase in home deliveries because they don't want to be seen schlepping down the street dragging the bags from the fancy pricing stores it's unseemly it's not that they're not buying it's not that they don't want to buy and some of them definitely are eager to buy to validate their status and position in life but on the other hand they don't want to be seen it's so you gotta give them good justification reason to buy the auto industry i've seen some dealers maybe there are some in your area seen a couple in my area who have gotten smart about this thing uh... and are doing a pretty good job if you stop to think about it if you go out right now by yourself a brand new uh... forty five thousand fifty thousand dollar gas guzzling climate destroying uh... uh... uh... s u v which i urge you to do uh... uh... uh... but uh... uh... but if you do uh... you might give pause to driving at home and putting it in a driveway if you know that your neighbor directly across the street who you know reasonably well lost his job three months ago and maybe headed towards foreclosure and the person who lives three doors down the street on the other side is also in economic trouble that might actually give you pause and so a message has to be crafted to make it okay to do the spending you would like to spend anyway in these dealers have gotten pretty good at uh... you know it's a it's a patriotic thing to buy this car you're preserving jobs here helping the factory we have here in the community so they've gotten pretty good at that message in terms of who you sell to you gotta think about who has who has the ability to buy no point really in trying to sell to people who don't who has the willingness to buy and then how can we help them along so there different customers are right at different times for different businesses you may be able to move up a little right now to a better breed of customer who is stepping down a little bit you may need to reach down a little bit but one way or another we can sell to someone different
